meta.title: Foundry Hosted-Agent Sample Finder
meta.description: A guided decision tree that turns the flat list of hosted-agent samples into a few answerable questions. Level 1 = where you're starting from (framework), Level 2 = how clients interact (protocol/channel), Level 3 = the capability you're adding.

start.title: Where are you starting from?
start.help: New here? Start with the recommended option. Already committed to a framework or have existing code? Jump straight to it.

options.label: I'm just getting started
options.description: New to Foundry hosted agents. Begin with the recommended first agents — one per framework — for the quickest path to something running.

options.label: I'm building on Microsoft Agent Framework
options.description: Microsoft's own agent framework, with the deepest, most native Foundry integration — sessions, tools, memory, and streaming built in. The best starting point for a brand-new agent.

options.label: I'm using LangGraph / LangChain
options.description: Microsoft officially supports the hosting integration through the langchain-azure-ai SDK — native sessions and streaming that bring your existing LangGraph graphs to Foundry.

options.label: I'm bringing a model SDK or another framework
options.description: Start native — call a Foundry model directly with just a model SDK, no agent framework — or bring the OpenAI Agents SDK, Anthropic Claude Agent SDK, GitHub Copilot SDK, or fully custom code, all hosted with the Foundry server SDKs.

notsure-result.intro: Not sure which framework yet? Start with the Agent Framework Basic agent — the tightest Foundry integration and the clearest learning path. Already on LangGraph? LangGraph Chat is the equivalent. Prefer to stay minimal in your own code? The Bring Your Own hello-world is the smallest possible start.

af-interaction.title: How will people or systems interact with your agent?
af-interaction.help: Level 2 — this picks the protocol/channel. Start with Responses if you're unsure; you can add other endpoints later.

options.label: Expose a standard, OpenAI-compatible API
options.description: Responses protocol — the platform manages conversation history, streaming, and background runs. Least code to write.

options.label: Expose a custom API you define and control
options.description: Invocations protocol — you design the request/response payload and manage sessions and streaming yourself. Maximum control.

options.label: Publish to Microsoft Teams / Microsoft 365
options.description: The standard API plus the Activity protocol, so your agent runs inside Microsoft Teams / Microsoft 365.

options.label: Delegate to, or be called by, other agents
options.description: The standard API plus the agent-to-agent (A2A) protocol for multi-agent workflows.

af-capability.title: What do you want your agent to do?
af-capability.help: Level 3 — the capability you're adding on top of a conversational agent.

options.label: Just the basics — chat and multi-turn conversation
options.description: Deploy your first agent and hold a conversation.

options.label: Use tools / connect to external systems
options.description: Local functions, remote MCP servers, or a Foundry Toolbox.

options.label: Ground answers in my knowledge (RAG, memory, skills)
options.description: Azure AI Search, Foundry IQ, persistent memory, or packaged skills.

options.label: Work with uploaded files
options.description: Manipulate and reason over files uploaded to the session.

options.label: Coordinate multiple agents or steps
options.description: Sequential workflows, declarative YAML workflows, or A2A delegation.

options.label: Harden it for production
options.description: Observability, managed-identity access, content safety, or optimization.

options.label: Automate a browser
options.description: Browse, scrape, and fill forms with the Browser Automation tool.

af-basic-result.intro: The Basic agent is your starting point — request/response plus multi-turn conversations via previous_response_id.

af-tools.title: What kind of tools?
af-tools.help: All three plug tools into a Responses agent — they differ in where the tools live.

options.label: Local Python functions I write
options.description: Register and invoke your own tool functions.

options.label: A remote MCP server
options.description: Consume tools from an external MCP provider (e.g. GitHub).

options.label: A managed Foundry Toolbox
options.description: Provision a toolbox and discover tools at runtime.

af-tools-local-result.intro: Register custom local tool functions alongside the LLM.

af-tools-mcp-result.intro: Connect to a remote MCP server to consume external tools and retrieval.

af-tools-toolbox-result.intro: Use a managed Foundry Toolbox for tool access.

af-knowledge.title: How do you want to bring in knowledge?
af-knowledge.help: Ground the agent in your own data or give it memory.

options.label: RAG over an Azure AI Search index
options.description: Classic retrieval-augmented generation grounded in indexed documents.

options.label: Agentic retrieval from a Foundry IQ knowledge base
options.description: A Foundry IQ knowledge base reached over a Foundry Toolbox MCP endpoint, keyless.

options.label: Persistent memory of user facts across sessions
options.description: Remember users between conversations with a Foundry Memory Store.

options.label: Packaged, file-based skills
options.description: Decouple tone/policy/procedures from code as reusable skills.

af-knowledge-rag-result.intro: Ground answers in documents indexed in Azure AI Search.

af-knowledge-iq-result.intro: Answer from a Foundry IQ knowledge base (Azure AI Search agentic retrieval) via a toolbox MCP endpoint.

af-knowledge-memory-result.intro: Persist semantic memory of user facts across sessions.

af-skills-result.intro: Two ways to package skills: native file-based skills bundled with the agent, or Foundry Skills managed via the REST API and downloaded at startup.

af-files-result.intro: Handle files uploaded to the session and reason over their contents.

af-orchestration.title: How do you want to coordinate work?
af-orchestration.help: From in-code pipelines to declarative workflows to cross-agent delegation.

options.label: Chain steps in code (sequential workflow)
options.description: Compose prompts/agents into a multi-step pipeline.

options.label: Author the workflow declaratively in YAML
options.description: A customer-support triage workflow defined entirely in YAML.

options.label: Delegate to another agent over A2A
options.description: A caller agent delegates to an executor exposed as an A2A endpoint.

af-orch-workflow-result.intro: Chain prompts/agents into a sequential orchestrated pipeline.

af-orch-declarative-result.intro: A multi-turn triage workflow authored in YAML that invokes specialist Foundry agents.

af-production.title: Which production concern?
af-production.help: Operational hardening and governance for a real deployment.

options.label: Observability — logging, metrics, tracing
options.description: Distributed traces and metrics to Application Insights.

options.label: Call Azure services with managed identity (no keys)
options.description: Blob Storage and Service Bus via per-agent Entra identity + RBAC.

options.label: Add a content-safety guardrail
options.description: Screen prompts and responses against a Responsible AI policy.

options.label: Optimize the agent's instructions and tools
options.description: Wire the agent for the Foundry Agent Optimizer (preview).

af-prod-observability-result.intro: Add logging, metrics, and distributed tracing to Application Insights.

af-prod-downstream-result.intro: Access Azure Blob Storage and Service Bus with the agent's per-agent identity — no connection strings.

af-prod-safety-result.intro: Attach a content-safety guardrail so the platform screens inputs and outputs.

af-prod-optimize-result.intro: A travel-approval agent wired for the Foundry Agent Optimizer (instructions, skills, tool descriptions).

af-browser-result.intro: Automate a real browser (browse, scrape, fill forms) via Foundry Toolbox + Azure Playwright Service.

af-inv-result.intro: Use the Invocations protocol for full HTTP control. Learn the handler/session pattern here, then port any Responses capability (tools, RAG, memory) — only the HTTP handler differs.

af-teams-result.intro: Publish the agent to Teams / M365 and answer Teams & calendar questions with Work IQ tools.

af-a2a-result.intro: A caller agent delegates over A2A to an executor exposed as an incoming A2A endpoint.

lg-interaction.title: How will clients interact with your LangGraph agent?
lg-interaction.help: Level 2 — protocol/channel for your existing LangGraph graph.

options.label: Expose a standard, OpenAI-compatible API
options.description: Responses protocol wired to your LangGraph graph — platform-managed sessions and streaming.

options.label: Expose a custom API you define and control
options.description: Invocations protocol with client-managed session state via a checkpointer — you own the payload and streaming.

options.label: Delegate to, or be called by, other agents
options.description: The standard API plus the agent-to-agent (A2A) protocol, between two LangGraph agents.

lg-capability.title: What do you want it to do?
lg-capability.help: Level 3 — the capability on top of a LangGraph Responses agent.

options.label: Basic chat with local tools
options.description: Minimal graph with get_current_time and calculator tools.

options.label: Use tools (Toolbox or MCP)
options.description: Foundry Toolbox (web_search + GitHub Copilot MCP) or a raw remote MCP server.

options.label: Work with uploaded files
options.description: Local filesystem tools plus a Toolbox code_interpreter.

options.label: Chain multiple LLM nodes (StateGraph workflow)
options.description: A custom StateGraph pipeline of specialized nodes.

options.label: Pause for human approval (human-in-the-loop)
options.description: Draft, then interrupt for approval before continuing.

options.label: Add observability / tracing
options.description: GenAI OpenTelemetry tracing to Application Insights.

lg-chat-result.intro: A minimal LangGraph chat agent with multi-turn conversation. lg-resp-chat uses the first-class langchain-azure-ai hosting integration; byo-resp-langgraph-chat hosts an equivalent graph with the generic BYO Responses server SDK.

lg-tools.title: Foundry Toolbox or a raw MCP server?
lg-tools.help: Both surface external tools to a LangGraph agent.

options.label: Foundry Toolbox (web_search + GitHub Copilot MCP)
options.description: Managed toolbox with OAuth consent surfacing.

options.label: A remote MCP server directly
options.description: Load tools from an MCP server via langchain_mcp_adapters.

lg-tools-toolbox-result.intro: A LangGraph agent wired to a Foundry Toolbox. lg-resp-toolbox uses the first-class integration; the BYO-hosted variants add a graph over the generic server SDK, including one that accesses tools on behalf of the signed-in user.

lg-tools-mcp-result.intro: A LangGraph agent that loads tools from a remote MCP server (default: GitHub Copilot MCP).

lg-files-result.intro: A LangGraph agent with filesystem tools and a Toolbox code_interpreter for uploaded files.

lg-workflows-result.intro: A custom StateGraph chaining slogan-writer, legal-reviewer, and formatter nodes.

lg-hitl-result.intro: A StateGraph that drafts a proposal and pauses for approval via interrupt.

lg-observability-result.intro: GenAI OpenTelemetry tracing with spans, metrics, and logs to Application Insights.

lg-inv-result.intro: A LangGraph agent over Invocations with client-managed session state. lg-inv-chat uses the first-class integration with a checkpointer; byo-inv-langgraph-chat hosts an equivalent graph with the generic BYO Invocations server SDK.

lg-a2a-result.intro: A concierge LangGraph agent delegates math questions over A2A to a math-expert agent.

byo-start.title: How will your agent interact with the world?
byo-start.help: Level 2 — this maps to the protocol your BYO agent speaks.

options.label: Expose a standard, OpenAI-compatible API
options.description: Responses protocol — any OpenAI-compatible client works; the platform manages sessions and streaming.

options.label: Expose a custom API you define and control
options.description: Invocations protocol — arbitrary JSON in / SSE out; you define the schema and manage sessions yourself.

byo-resp-capability.title: What's the closest starting point?
byo-resp-capability.help: Level 3 — pick the framework you already use, or the scenario nearest to yours.

options.label: Start native — just a model SDK, no framework
options.description: Write plain code that calls a Foundry model directly through a model SDK — no agent framework required. The simplest possible starting point.

options.label: Use the OpenAI Agents SDK
options.description: Host an agent built with the OpenAI Agents SDK over Responses.

options.label: Connect to tools via a Foundry Toolbox
options.description: Plain toolbox, or LangGraph + toolbox (incl. on-behalf-of user identity).

options.label: Take notes / maintain state with a custom tool
options.description: A note-taking agent.

options.label: Long-running background processing
options.description: Async background execution.

options.label: Manage sessions across multiple callers
options.description: Share one session id while history stays scoped per user.

options.label: Automate a browser
options.description: Toolbox MCP + playwright-cli for multi-session browsing.

options.label: Production concerns (secrets, optimization)
options.description: Connection-templated secrets, or wiring for the Agent Optimizer.

byo-resp-hello-result.intro: The simplest possible BYO Responses agent — plain code that calls a Foundry model directly through a model SDK, with no agent framework.

byo-resp-openai-result.intro: Host an OpenAI Agents SDK agent over the Responses protocol.

byo-resp-tools-result.intro: Wire a BYO agent to a Foundry Toolbox for external tools.

byo-resp-notetaking-result.intro: An agent that takes and retrieves notes with a custom tool.

byo-resp-background-result.intro: Long-running background processing with async execution.

byo-resp-session-result.intro: Multiple callers share one agent_session_id while history stays scoped per platform user.

byo-resp-browser-result.intro: Browser automation with Toolbox MCP session lifecycle and playwright-cli.

options.label: Resolve secrets from connections via env vars
options.description: Connection-templated secret resolver with a kind-aware safety policy.

options.label: Optimize the agent (Agent Optimizer)
options.description: Minimal or customer-support agent wired for the optimizer.

byo-resp-envvars-result.intro: Read env vars injected by Foundry's connection-templated secret resolver.

byo-resp-optimization-result.intro: BYO agents wired for the Foundry Agent Optimizer — start with hello-world, then the customer-support scenario.

byo-inv-capability.title: What's your Invocations scenario?
byo-inv-capability.help: Level 3 — arbitrary JSON in / SSE out; you own the payload and sessions.

options.label: Start native — just a model SDK, no framework
options.description: Write plain code that calls a Foundry model directly through a model SDK — no agent framework required. JSON in, streaming SSE out.

options.label: Use the Claude Agent SDK
options.description: Host an agent built with the Claude Agent SDK over Invocations.

options.label: Connect to tools via a Foundry Toolbox
options.description: Toolbox MCP endpoint over Invocations.

options.label: Take notes / maintain state
options.description: A note-taking agent over Invocations.

options.label: A custom UI streaming protocol (AG-UI)
options.description: AG-UI streaming via the Invocations endpoint.

options.label: Bridge to GitHub Copilot
options.description: Use GitHub Copilot as the AI backbone.

options.label: Pause for human approval (human-in-the-loop)
options.description: Long-running agent that waits for approval.

options.label: Trigger from an event (Azure Event Grid)
options.description: Storage → Event Grid → agent, summarizing new blobs.

options.label: Diagnose networking / infra (no LLM)
options.description: DNS/TCP/TLS/HTTP probes for BYO network debugging.

byo-inv-hello-result.intro: The simplest BYO Invocations agent — plain code that calls a Foundry model directly through a model SDK (no framework); arbitrary JSON in, streaming SSE out.

byo-inv-claude-result.intro: A getting-started Claude Agent SDK agent with streaming over Invocations.

byo-inv-toolbox-result.intro: A BYO Invocations agent wired to a Foundry Toolbox MCP endpoint.

byo-inv-notetaking-result.intro: A note-taking agent using the Invocations protocol.

byo-inv-agui-result.intro: An agent using the AG-UI streaming protocol via the Invocations endpoint.

byo-inv-copilot-result.intro: An agent that integrates with GitHub Copilot as the AI backbone.

byo-inv-hitl-result.intro: A long-running agent that pauses for human approval before continuing.

byo-inv-eventgrid-result.intro: Event-driven: Storage → Event Grid → agent; summarizes a new blob back to Storage.

byo-inv-diagnostic-result.intro: A no-LLM agent that probes DNS/TCP/TLS/HTTP and reports what the runtime can reach.
